title: INF-9 LoRA-for-SSM (LoRA mount/unmount for Mamba2 + RWKV)
goal: Implement LoraStackOps for SSM/RWKV variants: LoRA mount/unmount that targets SSM-specific weight tensors (Mamba2 conv1d weights, in_proj, out_proj, A/D parameters; RWKV time-mix and channel-mix weights). PEFT semantics adapted to non-attention architectures.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/candle/ssm_lora.rs implementing LoraStackOps for SSM variants; mamba2.rs + rwkv_v5.rs + rwkv_v6.rs + rwkv_v7.rs forward wrappers to apply LoRA delta; tests.
unblock_conditions: MT-084 transformer-LoRA + MT-085..MT-087 SSM/RWKV base merged.
implementation_notes: Per refinement INF-9 feature_parity_detail: "LoRA mount/unmount for SSMs (LoRA-for-SSM work)". This is research-frontier territory — LoRA-for-SSM is less standardised than LoRA-for-transformer. Target modules per architecture: Mamba2 = `{in_proj, x_proj, dt_proj, out_proj}` (the Linear projections; SSM A/D/conv parameters are NOT good LoRA targets due to scalar/diagonal structure); RWKV v5/v6/v7 = `{ time_mix.*.weight, channel_mix.*.weight }` (similar to attention/mlp split). LoraDescriptor for SSM models requires `architecture: ArchitectureTag` field; mount validates target_modules against the loaded model's architecture. PEFT scaling formula same as transformer: y = base.forward(x) + scaling * strength * (B @ (A @ x)). Per AC-CANDLE-ADAPTER capabilities update after this MT lands: supports_lora=true for SSM variants. Tests (env-gated): mount a small fixture LoRA on Mamba2 (operator-supplied), generate, assert output differs; unmount, output reverts. Note: very few PRODUCTION-quality LoRAs exist for Mamba2/RWKV in 2026 — test MAY fixture-generate a small random LoRA for the smoke test.
risk_if_missed: INF-9 LoRA parity entry missing; operator E-2 acceptance breached.
validator_focus: Architecture tag validation; target-module names per architecture; ArchitectureTag field carried through LoraDescriptor.

minimum_controls: Per-architecture target-module name list explicit (Mamba2 vs RWKV).
